objective: Close the remaining F-0049 race by moving the active-organization recheck under the existing shared organization-scope lock and into the same transaction as the shared account-phone identity lock, account writes and redacted success audit. Preserve stable conflict/not-found responses and failed audits without expanding roles, permissions, entitlement, schema or API capability.
blockedFiles: role, permission, authorization or entitlement expansion
schema, migration or persistent database execution
Provider calls, external services, deployment, PR or force-push

authoritativeChainEvidence.uiApi: AdminOpsManagement sends an explicit organizationPublicId through the existing POST /api/v1/admin-accounts contract; F-0046 already made that selection complete and explicit.
authoritativeChainEvidence.handlerService: admin-flow-runtime already validates creation permission, maps repository phone conflicts and organization_not_found to stable responses, and writes redacted failed audits; no service or API contract change is needed.
authoritativeChainEvidence.repository: admin-flow-runtime-repository already locks the shared phone identity and writes account rows plus the redacted success audit atomically, but its active organization lookup remains outside that transaction.
authoritativeChainEvidence.schemaMigration: organization status and account relations already exist; the shared organization-scope advisory lock is the authoritative concurrency boundary, so no schema or migration change is required.
authoritativeChainEvidence.tests: Focused repository tests prove organization scope lock then active recheck then phone identity lock and writes, including fail-closed missing/inactive organization behavior; existing phone-lock, lifecycle and admin regressions cover unchanged conflict/audit semantics.
authoritativeChainEvidence.nonClaims: No role, permission, entitlement, schema, migration, persistent database execution, dependency, Provider, external service or mechanism change.
